expectations.text: Output shows all 3 stages: finder scan, adversary challenges, and referee ruling
expectations.evidence: No 3-stage structure — single-pass review organized by severity (Critical/High/Medium)

expectations.text: Finder identifies SQL injection via string interpolation in the query
expectations.evidence: Issue 1: SQL Injection Vulnerability identified with fix example

expectations.text: Finder identifies MD5 as insecure for password hashing
expectations.evidence: Issue 2: Use of MD5 for Password Hashing identified

expectations.text: Finder identifies the token generation as predictable/insecure
Issues have IDs (F-001, F-002, etc.) with severity ratings
expectations.evidence: Issues numbered 1-11 but no F-### format; severity is Critical/High/Medium, not BLOCKER/WARNING/SUGGESTION

expectations.text: Adversary issues KILL/SURVIVE/DISPUTED verdicts for each finding
expectations.evidence: No adversary stage — no challenging of findings

expectations.text: Referee produces a final issue list with definitive severities
expectations.evidence: No referee — no independent verification or severity adjustments

expectations.text: Final verdict is NEEDS CHANGES (not APPROVED) given the security issues
expectations.evidence: Summary says 'unsuitable for production use' but no formal APPROVED/NEEDS CHANGES verdict

expectations.text: Debate metrics include finder accuracy % and false positive rate %
